# emu/macppc — SheepShaver assets

Everything in this directory except this README is **git-ignored**. Drop your
own files here; `tools/setup_macppc.sh` will look for them and wire up a
SheepShaver config.

## Required files

| File | What it is | Where to get it |
|---|---|---|
| `rom` | New World Mac ROM (~4 MB) | dump from your own hardware, or the "Mac OS ROM Update 1.0" extract |
| `install.iso` | Mac OS 8.1 – 9.0.4 install CD image | your own media (e.g. `~/Downloads/MacOS8_1.iso`) |

## Generated by setup / first run

| File | What it is |
|---|---|
| `boot.dsk` | 500 MB HFS volume — you install Mac OS onto this on first boot |
| `.sheepshaver_prefs` | regenerated by `tools/run_macppc.sh` on every launch |
| `.sheepshaver_nvram` | emulator NVRAM state |
| `shared/` | host folder mounted as the "Unix" desktop volume inside the guest |

## Workflow

```sh
tools/setup_macppc.sh ~/Downloads/MacOS8_1.iso   # one-time
make run-macppc                                  # build + launch, every time
```

First launch boots from the CD; run the installer onto **MacOS** (the blank
`boot.dsk`). After that, `make run-macppc` boots straight to the desktop with
a fresh **Relic** volume containing the just-built app.

## Networking (one-time, inside the guest)

`tools/run_macppc.sh` already sets `ether slirp`, so the emulated Mac has a NAT'd
Ethernet card. You just have to tell Mac OS to use it:

1. **Apple menu → Control Panels → TCP/IP**
2. Connect via: **Ethernet**, Configure: **Using DHCP Server**
3. Close the window, click **Save**.

slirp hands out `10.0.2.15` (gateway `10.0.2.2`, DNS `10.0.2.3`). Outbound
TCP/UDP only — `ping` won't work, but Relic's API calls will.

If the TCP/IP panel has no **Ethernet** option, your OS install skipped Open
Transport: re-run the installer from the CD, choose **Custom Install**, and add
**Open Transport** + **Internet Access**.
